questions.question: In Python's OOP model, what is the relationship between a class and an instance?
choices: A class is a copy of an instance
A class is a factory that generates instances
An instance is a blueprint for a class
Classes and instances are the same thing
questions.explanation: Classes serve as factories for generating instances. Each instance is created from a class and inherits its attributes, but can also have its own unique data.

questions.question: The process by which a subclass receives attributes from a superclass is called ___.
questions.explanation: Inheritance allows subclasses to reuse and extend the behavior defined in their superclasses, forming a class tree hierarchy.

questions.question: What does polymorphism mean in the context of Python OOP?
choices: A variable can only hold one type of data
The same operation can behave differently depending on the object it acts upon
All classes must implement the same methods
Objects cannot change their type at runtime
questions.explanation: Polymorphism means that the meaning of an operation depends on the object being operated on. Different objects can respond to the same method call in different ways.

questions.question: In a Python class tree, how does attribute lookup work when you access an attribute on an instance?
choices: Python only looks at the instance itself
Python searches the instance first, then its class, then any superclasses from bottom to top
Python searches the superclass first, then the subclass, then the instance
Python searches all classes in alphabetical order
questions.explanation: Python's attribute lookup follows the inheritance tree from bottom to top: it searches the instance first, then its class, then superclasses, stopping at the first place the attribute is found.

questions.question: The OOP principle of bundling data and the methods that operate on that data into a single unit is called ___.
questions.explanation: Encapsulation wraps data and behavior together inside objects. In Python, this is achieved through classes, though Python does not enforce strict access control like some other languages.

questions.question: Why does Python use OOP?
choices: It is the only programming paradigm Python supports
OOP enables code reuse through inheritance, reduces redundancy, and helps manage program complexity
OOP makes Python programs run faster than procedural code
Python requires all code to be inside classes
questions.explanation: OOP's main benefits in Python are code reuse through inheritance, reduced redundancy by factoring common behavior into superclasses, and better management of program complexity through modular design.

questions.question: In a class tree, what is a superclass?
choices: A class that has no parent classes
A class that is higher in the tree and provides attributes inherited by classes below it
A class that cannot be instantiated
A class that overrides all methods from its children
questions.explanation: A superclass (also called a parent or base class) sits higher in the class tree. Classes below it (subclasses) inherit its attributes and can customize or extend them.
